mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.1% as of Aug-2011), the persistent absence of a positive real risk-free alternative forces yield-seeking capital into equities, producing a structural positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the near-zero rate regime persists.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS<=0.25% periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window while FEDFUNDS remains near zero.

mechanism_narrative: When month-over-month PAYEMS growth decelerates and crosses below a near-stagnation threshold (net job gains below +50k for two consecutive prints), it signals fragile labor demand consistent with recession risk, which pressures forward equity returns negatively over the following 20 trading days as growth expectations reprice downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly net change < 50k for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following triggered weak-payroll episodes are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the signal fails to produce Rank IC above 0.03 across a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in INDPRO growth and RSAFS growth indicates that both production and final demand are contracting together, a joint condition more predictive of an impending broad economic slowdown than either series alone, and this combined deceleration drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage 94.04 Aug-2011, RSAFS vintage 389502 Aug-2011
failure_condition: When both conditions are jointly triggered, 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction signal's Rank IC does not exceed the sum of the two single-series IC contributions by a meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-financial-crisis recovery regime, a sustained month-over-month increase in HOUST reflects re-emerging confidence in household balance sheets and construction activity, which leads equity markets by signaling durable consumption recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter specifically during recovery phases.
failure_condition: During periods identified as post-crisis recovery, rising HOUST trends fail to produce 60-day forward returns exceeding the unconditional base rate by a statistically significant margin (p>0.05), or the relationship reverses sign outside recovery regimes without being flagged as regime-conditional.

mechanism_narrative: Stable, low year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation readings (below the Fed's implicit tolerance band) remove pressure for premature monetary tightening, anchoring low discount rate expectations and supporting a persistent modest positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during low-and-stable CPI periods (YoY change < 3%) are not significantly different from returns during high-inflation periods, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across multiple 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below a 1% annualized threshold over two consecutive quarterly releases, it signals elevated recession probability that is not yet fully priced by equity markets, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as growth downgrades propagate to earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized QoQ growth < 1% for 2 consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage_value=13260.5 vintage_date=2011-04-01, large revision risk noted
failure_condition: Following triggered sub-1% GDP growth episodes, 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the signal's predictive power disappears once GDPC1 data revisions are incorporated (revised vs vintage divergence exceeds 20%).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is at the zero lower bound and UNRATE simultaneously remains above 9%, markets price in a high probability of additional unconventional Fed easing (a 'Fed put'), and this joint condition produces a short-term positive S&P 500 return over the next week as anticipatory buying occurs ahead of policy announcements.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.1% and UNRATE=9.1% as of Aug-2011
regime_hint: zero lower bound with persistent labor slack
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during joint zero-rate/high-unemployment episodes show no significant positive skew versus baseline (p>0.05), or the effect reverses following confirmed Fed non-action announcements.

mechanism_narrative: During a demand-recovery regime following a financial crisis, sustained month-over-month acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) signals strengthening consumer spending that has not yet been fully reflected in equity prices, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month specifically in recovery-phase conditions.
failure_condition: During identified recovery regimes, months with accelerating RSAFS growth fail to show 20-day forward returns exceeding the regime baseline by a statistically significant margin (p>0.05), or the sign of the effect flips in non-recovery regimes without regime conditioning being detected.
